parts.title: Part 1
parts.content:
Questions 1-10

Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. FAMILY EXCURSIONS Cruise on a lake Example Travel on an old ….. steamship ….. parts.title: Part 2
parts.content:
Questions 11-14

Choose the correct letter, A , B or C . Talk to new kitchen assistants

Questions 15-16

Choose TWO letters, A-E .

Questions 17-20

What is the responsibility of each of the following restaurant staff? Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F , next to Questions Responsibilities Restaurant staff

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 11-14

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Talk to new kitchen assistants

contentBlocks.prompt: According to the manager, what do most people like about the job of kitchen assistant?
options: A. the variety of work
B. the friendly atmosphere
C. the opportunities for promotion

contentBlocks.prompt: The manager is concerned about some of the new staff’s
options: A. jewellery.
B. hair styles.
C. shoes.

contentBlocks.prompt: The manager says that the day is likely to be busy for kitchen staff because
options: A. it is a public holiday.
B. the head chef is absent.
C. the restaurant is almost fully booked.

contentBlocks.prompt: Only kitchen staff who are 18 or older are allowed to use
options: A. the waste disposal unit.
B. the electric mixer.
C. the meat slicer.

contentBlocks.prompt: According to the manager, which TWO things can make the job of kitchen assistant stressful?
options: A. They have to follow orders immediately.
B. The kitchen gets very hot.
C. They may not be able to take a break.
D. They have to do overtime.
E. The work is physically demanding.

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 17-20

What is the responsibility of each of the following restaurant staff?

Choose FOUR answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-F, next to Questions

Responsibilities

Restaurant staff

options: A. training courses
B. food stocks
C. first aid
D. breakages
E. staff discounts
F. timetables

parts.title: Part 3
parts.content:
Questions 21-23

Choose the correct letter, A , B or C . Paper on Public Libraries

Questions 24-30

Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer. Study of local library: possible questions whether it has a [24] of its own its policy regarding noise of various kinds how it’s affected by laws regarding all aspects of [25] how the design needs to take the [26] of customers into account what [27] is required in case of accidents why a famous person’s [28] is located in the library whether it has a [29] of local organisations how it’s different from a library in a [30]

contentBlocks.text:
Questions 21-23

Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

Paper on Public Libraries

contentBlocks.prompt: What will be the main topic of Trudie and Stewart’s paper?
options: A. how public library services are organised in different countries
B. how changes in society are reflected in public libraries
C. how the funding of public libraries has changed

contentBlocks.prompt: They agree that one disadvantage of free digitalised books is that
options: A. they may take a long time to read.
B. they can be difficult to read.
C. they are generally old.

contentBlocks.prompt: Stewart expect that in the future libraries will
options: A. maintain their traditional function.
B. become centres for local communities.
C. no longer contain any books.

parts.title: Part 4
parts.content:
Questions 31-40

Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer. Four business values Many business values can result in [31] . Senior managers need to understand and deal with the potential [32] that may result. Collaboration During a training course, the speaker was in a team that had to build a [33] . Other teams experienced [34] from trying to collaborate. The speaker’s team won because they reduced collaboration. Sales of a [35] were poor because of collaboration. Industriousness Hard work may be a bad use of various company [36] . The word ‘lazy’ in this context refers to people who avoid doing tasks that are [37] . Creativity An advertising campaign for a [38] was memorable but failed to boost sales. Creativity should be used as a response to a particular [39] . Excellence According to one study, on average, pioneers had a [40] that was far higher than that of followers. Companies that always aim at excellence may miss opportunities.
